ops.title: DECISION (2026-07-18): daemonkit Go unix-socket trust ships best-effort; T1 TOCTOU accepted, XPC deferred
ops.body:
**Decision (user, 2026-07-18):** "Accept + document now, XPC later" for the confirmed T1 LOCAL_PEERTOKEN query-time TOCTOU (see verified-findings note 97c5f2a5).

daemonkit's Go daemons keep unix sockets + codesign peer verification (trust.Policy: same-UID floor + DR-pinned Team+identifier via LOCAL_PEERTOKEN → Security.framework). This is shipped as **best-effort defense-in-depth on top of the same-UID floor**, with the residual same-UID TOCTOU documented honestly in trust/ (LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is query-time, not connection-bound; a same-UID attacker can race an exec-into-trusted-binary before the daemon's token fetch — same-UID is the fleet threat model, but the DR pin still raises the bar substantially over UID-only).

The sound primitive for connection-bound code identity is a kernel per-message audit trailer (XPC/Mach). The Swift↔Swift links already use it (NSXPCConnection.setCodeSigningRequirement). A cgo-free Go XPC/Mach transport is filed as deferred follow-up (task: "Go XPC/Mach audit-trailer transport…"), revisited when a surface needs connection-bound identity — NOT a Track F blocker.

Rationale: closes the plan's spike exit criterion (second security review done + XPC alternative named); keeps F→I→P→K→S on schedule; the residual risk is a narrow high-effort local race by an already-same-UID actor, and fail-closed + the DR pin remain enforced. T2 (library-validation + get-task-allow rejection) is being fixed regardless.
